2606.21144.title: AdaMem: Learning What to Remember for Personalized Long-Horizon LLM Agents
2606.21144.paper_summary: Long-term memory systems for Large Language Model (LLM) agents typically try to \emph{remember everything}, extracting memories uniformly to retain as many facts as possible. In production, however, inference cost and finite context budgets make this untenable: beyond consolidating raw dialogue into memory, an agent must exert \emph{write control}, efficiently keeping only the information each user actually cares about. Otherwise, long-horizon personalized interactions suffer \emph{memory bloat}, where irrelevant trivia crowds out useful information and steadily erodes question-answering (QA) accuracy. We argue that what is worth remembering is role-dependent, and propose \textbf{AdaMem} (Adaptive Memory), a method that \emph{learns what to remember} for each user from feedback. AdaMem maintains a structured, role-specific Memory Policy and refines it from weekly QA feedback through a lightweight, patch-style self-reflection step with failure rollback. To study this setting, we build \textbf{AdaMem-Bench}, a benchmark that simulates weeks of interaction with week-by-week QA. Across two extraction models and two feedback modes, AdaMem improves QA accuracy by up to \textbf{+9.0\%} over the uniform Mem0 baseline while shrinking memory volume by \textbf{9\%}.
2606.21144.paper_summary_zh: 長期記憶系統對於大型語言模型（LLM）代理通常試圖\emph{記住所有事物}，均勻地提取記憶以保留盡可能多的事實。然而，在實際應用中，推理成本和有限的上下文預算使這變得不可行：除了將原始對話整合到記憶中，代理還必須施加\emph{寫入控制}，有效地僅保留每個用戶實際關心的信息。否則，長期個性化互動會遭受\emph{記憶膨脹}，無關的瑣事會擠占有用信息，並穩步侵蝕問答（QA）準確性。我們認為，值得記住的內容是依賴於角色的，並提出\textbf{AdaMem}（自適應記憶），這是一種\emph{學習記住什麼}的每個用戶的反饋方法。AdaMem維護一個結構化的、特定角色的記憶政策，並通過每週的QA反饋進行精煉，使用輕量級的、補丁式的自我反思步驟，並具有失敗回滾功能。為了研究這一設定，我們建立了\textbf{AdaMem-Bench}，這是一個基準，模擬了幾週的互動，並逐週進行QA。在兩個提取模型和兩個反饋模式下，AdaMem將QA準確性提高了最多\textbf{+9.0\%}，同時將記憶量縮減了\textbf{9\%}。
2606.21144.author: Xingyu Chen et.al.
2606.21144.authors: Xingyu Chen, Rui Wang, Zhaopeng Tu, Liefeng Bo
